pages.raw_text:
Announcement Is made that the
Panhandle smelter will resume op.
erations at Standpoint, April 1. In
the meantime additional machinery
will be installed and improvements
made that will call for an outlay of
100000.


D. M. DrumhelIer, J. X. Smith and
M. J. O'Neill of Spokane have pur-
chased $40 acres near MabtOn,
Wash, which they will plant in
apple and peach trees and plat into
small tracts.


The National Feed and Mill Co.
will soon occupy their new building
at the intersection of Perry street
and the N. P. tracks, built at cost
of $4,000. The present building oc-
cupied by the company near the
Schade brewery has been purchased
by the Milwaukee railroad


Bliss N. Davis, connected with
the fiscal end of the postoffice de-
partment on the Pacific coast, as
signed to salaries and allowances,
is in Spokane.
